---
title: "Thales Maia, PhD"
description-meta: "Quarto Academic Website Template adapted by Dr. Gang He"
image: "files/images/foto.jpg"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 70%
  links:
    - text: "{{< ai lattes >}} Lattes"
      href: http://lattes.cnpq.br/8637504941507702
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"   # use academic icons, "" are needed, you also need to install the fontawesome extension
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7943-6322
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} Research Gate"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Thales-Maia
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com.br/citations?user=7oeMSpAAAAAJ&hl=pt-BR&oi=ao
      target: _blank
    - text: '<img src="files/icons/somos-plus.png" style="height: 1.1em; vertical-align: -0.2em; margin-right: 0.3em;"> UFMG'
      href: https://somos.ufmg.br/professor/thales-alexandre-carvalho-maia
      target: _blank
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/thales-maia-38755a23
      target: _blank
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/thalesmaia
      target: _blank
    - icon: youtube
      text: YouTube
      href: https://www.youtube.com/@thalesmaia6607
      target: _blank
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:thalesmaia@gmail.com
listing:   # Recent news and events
  - id: recent-news
    contents: 
      - backend/news
    exclude:
      title: "News and Events"
    # type: table
    type: grid             # Mudamos para grid para permitir itens lado a lado
    grid-columns: 2        # Força a ter exatamente 2 colunas
    max-items: 4           # Limita para mostrar no máximo 4 notícias
    page-size: 4           # Garante que a "página" tenha apenas 4 itens
    # max-items: 3
    # page-size: 3
    fields: [date, title]
    sort-ui: false     # Remove a caixa "Order By"
    filter-ui: false   # Remove a caixa "Filter"
    # max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
      

  - id: recent-blog
    contents: 
      - backend/blog
    exclude:
      title: ""
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
    page-size: 3
    fields: [image, date, title, subtitle]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

:::{#hero-heading}
**Developing Technology for the Industrial Sector | Electric Machine Design, Power Generation & Automation | Professor**

<small>Belo Horizonte, Minas Gerais, Brasil <img src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f4/Bandeira_de_Minas_Gerais.svg" style="height: 1em; vertical-align: -0.1em;"> 🇧🇷</small>

I develop technology to overcome industrial challenges, working directly in the renewable generation, carbon chain, oil and gas, and automotive sectors. With over 20 years of market experience—combined with my background as a University Professor—I have built a solid foundation in designing electric machines, electronics, instrumentation, and software, creating complete architectures for processes, drives, and automation.

My main specialty is developing custom generators and motors (axial flux, high-speed, and in-wheel motors), integrating them with power electronics, control systems, and industrial communication stacks. I have led projects involving wind power for rural grids, microturbines, hybrid mobile machines, and special powertrains for harsh environments.

In parallel, I have extensive practical experience in process and thermal systems instrumentation: measuring combustion gases in carbonization furnaces, gasification, biochar production, and industrial dryers, integrating sensors, signal conditioning, PLCs, and embedded systems.

I am usually called when the problem is non-trivial: noisy environments, weak electrical grids, severe thermal conditions, legacy equipment, or ambitious energy targets. I solve the challenge by developing your system, validating it through models and prototypes, and then supporting its implementation and commissioning.

If you are looking for someone with a systemic view for your project—capable of connecting everything from the chemistry of your process and the behavior of your machines to the code of your controllers—and turning it all into a robust architecture, that is exactly where I generate the most value.

:::


## News and Events
Check out the latest &nbsp;[News](backend/news/index.qmd#category=news)&nbsp;, &nbsp;[Events](backend/news/index.qmd#category=event)&nbsp;, and &nbsp;[More »](backend/news/index.qmd)

:::{#recent-news}
:::

## Blog
Check out the latest &nbsp;[Posts](backend/blog/index.qmd)&nbsp; and &nbsp;[More »](/backend/blog/index.qmd)

:::{#recent-blog}
:::

<p align="right">[All Posts »](/backend/blog/index.qmd)</p>
